summary: OpenNori now provides one first-time setup path and a simple project init path: setup plan previews Codex Plugin marketplace/add, packaged Skills, global CLI install, project .opennori initialization, and doctor; init previews/creates project state; README, website, Plugin metadata, nori/project-health Skills, protocol, and tests use npx opennori setup plus opennori init as the user-facing path.

sources.label: tmp=$(mktemp -d); node ./bin/opennori.js setup --root "$tmp" --json; node ./bin/opennori.js init --root "$tmp" --confirm --json; node ./bin/opennori.js doctor --root "$tmp" --json
sources.command: tmp=$(mktemp -d); node ./bin/opennori.js setup --root "$tmp" --json; node ./bin/opennori.js init --root "$tmp" --confirm --json; node ./bin/opennori.js doctor --root "$tmp" --json

reviewability: Run npm run test, run setup/init in a temporary project, inspect setup_plan actions for codex plugin, packaged_skills, global_cli, project_state, and doctor, then read README/website/Skills for the same user path.
limitations: Tests mock confirmed external Codex/npm setup writes; the temp smoke verifies preview and project init without mutating global Codex/npm state. A real first-time user will still need to approve setup in their own environment.
